name: Taj Mahal
description: An ivory-white marble mausoleum built by Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal, celebrated as a symbol of eternal love and Mughal architecture.

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
description: A historic Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi, famed for its towering, intricately sculpted gopurams covered in thousands of colourful figures.

name: Amber Fort
description: A majestic hilltop fort of honey-hued sandstone and white marble, known for its artistic Hindu-style elements and the dazzling Sheesh Mahal.

name: Kerala Backwaters
description: A network of tranquil lagoons, lakes and canals lined with coconut palms, best explored aboard a traditional houseboat at sunset.

name: Rishikesh River Rafting
description: The Ganges rushes through the Himalayan foothills here, making Rishikesh the country's white-water rafting and bungee-jumping capital.

name: Goa Beaches
description: Golden sands, swaying palms and a laid-back Indo-Portuguese charm make Goa's coastline India's favourite beach getaway.

name: National Museum
description: One of the largest museums in the country, holding over 200,000 artefacts spanning 5,000 years of Indian art, archaeology and culture.

name: Golden Temple
description: The holiest Gurdwara of Sikhism, its gold-plated sanctum reflecting in the surrounding Amrit Sarovar, welcomes visitors of every faith.

name: Hawa Mahal
description: The 'Palace of Winds', a five-storey pink sandstone facade with 953 small windows built to let royal women observe street life unseen.

name: Ranthambore National Park
description: A former royal hunting ground turned tiger reserve, where ancient ruins sit scattered through dry deciduous forest and lakes.

name: Mehrangarh Fort
description: One of India's largest forts, its massive walls rise from a rocky outcrop above the 'Blue City', housing an outstanding museum of Rajput heritage.

name: Varanasi Ghats
description: Ancient stone steps along the Ganges where pilgrims bathe at dawn and evening aarti ceremonies fill the riverfront with light and chant.
